name: Varunprasaath R. Selvaraju, Ph.D.
summary: PhD chemist/materials scientist with 6+ years building structure-property-processing datasets across organic, polymer, and thin-film material systems. Experienced in solid-state-adjacent characterization, thermal/spectroscopic analysis, stability/degradation studies, DOE/SPC, and technical reports that connect material attributes to process and formulation decisions.

education.details:
Dissertation: Modular Design and Electrochemical Properties of Xanthene-Based Electrochromic Materials
Advisor: Prof. Seth Marder
(Transferred from Georgia Institute of Technology)

education.degree: B.S. (Research), Major: Chemistry; Minor: Materials Science
education.details: INSPIRE Scholarship for Higher Education (Top 1% nationally)

bullets: Lead DOE-guided process-development experiments for bio-based polymer systems, defining material-quality readouts, process windows, reproducibility criteria, and SOP-ready specifications for scale-up.
Use FT-IR, thermal analysis, spectroscopy, and statistical process-control concepts to connect process parameters with material stability, quality attributes, and method repeatability.

Designed, synthesized, purified, and characterized 15+ organic electrochromic material variants using UV-Vis-NIR, FT-IR, cyclic voltammetry, spectroelectrochemistry, NMR, GPC, TGA, DSC, and mass-spectrometry-informed analysis.
Mapped structure-property-stability relationships, degradation pathways, and performance failure modes to guide molecular design and analytical interpretation.
Co-led AFOSR-funded molecular thin-film process development, linking CVD/surface preparation to morphology, interfaces, optical performance, and reproducible process controls.
Optimized polymer thin-film deposition using DOE strategies and in-situ QCM-D process monitoring, characterizing film-growth kinetics and coating reproducibility.

Managed characterization/process-development lab operations for a 15-member group; implemented equipment tracking, calibration routines, SOPs, and safety protocols that improved instrument uptime by 30% and strengthened data integrity.

projects.title: Pharmaceutical Materials Characterization Bridge
projects.description: Translate organic/materials chemistry evidence into API/excipient, polymorph, crystallization, amorphous-solid-dispersion, and physical-stability problem solving.
projects.tech: FT-IR, UV-Vis-NIR, NMR, GPC/SEC, MS-informed analysis, TGA/DSC, DOE

projects.title: Analytical Stability and Degradation Study
projects.description: Identified degradation, redox instability, and performance failure modes across functional material variants using orthogonal analytical characterization.
projects.tech: UV-Vis-NIR, FT-IR, CV, TGA/DSC, NMR, GPC, MS-informed analysis

projects.title: DOE-Guided Polymer Process Development
projects.description: Defined process windows, analytical readouts, reproducibility criteria, and SOP-ready specifications for scalable polymer systems.
projects.tech: DOE, SPC, FT-IR, thermal analysis, technical reports

publications: Ramasamy Selvaraju, V.; Venta, R.; Rajapaksha, I.; Zhang, J.; Barlow, S.; Salman, S.; Scott, C.; Marder, S. "Narrow-Band Electrochromism of a Ferrocene-Substituted Rhodamine B." Chemistry of Materials, 2025, 37, 5558–5568.

Mandal, J.; Ramasamy Selvaraju, V.; Yan, W.; Divandari, M.; Spencer, N. D.; Dubner, M. "In Situ Monitoring of SI-ATRP Throughout Multiple Reinitiations Under Flow by Means of a Quartz Crystal Microbalance." RSC Advances, 2018, 8, 20048–20055.

Ramasamy Selvaraju, V.; Venta, R.; Rajapaksha, I.; Zhang, J.; Barlow, S.; Salman, S.; Scott, C.; Marder, S. "Beyond Rhodamine B: Investigating Alternative Xanthene Cores for Modular Electrochromic Systems." (Manuscript under preparation).

Electrochemical and Spectroscopic Properties of Substituted Rhodamine Derivatives, Gerischer-Lewerenz Electrochemistry Symposium, Fort Collins, CO, August 2024.

skills.Materials & Solid-State Characterization: FT-IR, UV-Vis-NIR, ellipsometry, NMR, GPC/SEC, GC-MS/LC-MS exposure, QCM-D, TGA, DSC, cyclic voltammetry; XRD/SEM-adjacent familiarity
skills.Pharmaceutical Materials Framing: Crystallization/polymorph/salt-selection concepts, physical stability, drug-excipient compatibility mindset, amorphous-solid-dispersion awareness, structure-property-processing analysis
skills.Process & Statistical Methods: DOE, SPC, JMP/basic, Excel, Python/basic, process windows, reproducibility studies, SOP-ready specifications
skills.Documentation & Communication: Technical reports, scientific publications, data packages, cross-functional presentations, lab notebooks, regulatory-documentation readiness

honors: Plastic Sustainability Innovation in Material Award
